name: Freila and Zújar Loop via Negratín Reservoir
name_original: Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar
description: Cycle a moderate 15.3-mile (24.7 km) loop around Embalse de Negratín from Freila, enjoying turquoise waters and reddish mountains.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Freila traverse a landscape characterized by small, fertile valleys that contrast with reddish wastelands and semi-desert badlands. The region features the expansive blue waters of the Negratín reservoir, surrounded by geological formations and arid, colorful hillsides. Routes often pass through olive groves and fruit trees in fertile meadows, transitioning to deeply eroded, desert-like terrain. This diverse environment provides varied conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Freila The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar, a 15.4 miles (24.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Negratín reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hiking Cerro Jabalcón from Zújar around the Embalse de Negratín, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.1 km) path. This route includes ascents around Cerro Jabalcón and views of the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Los Coloraos Desert – Badlands of Gorafe loop from Gorafe, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail leading through desert badlands, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Freila is defined by reddish wastelands, the Negratín reservoir, and fertile valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Freila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 79 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Freila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Freila?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Freila, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 79 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Freila?
A: Freila's touring cycling routes feature a dramatically varied landscape. You'll encounter small, fertile valleys with olive groves and fruit trees, contrasting sharply with reddish wastelands and semi-desert 'badlands'. The expansive blue waters of the Negratín reservoir are a dominant feature, often surrounded by these arid, colorful hillsides, creating a visually stunning and geographically diverse environment.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Freila?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041882129/embalse-de-negratin-circular-desde-freila-por-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.4-mile (24.7 km) route that offers beautiful views of the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Desert – Badlands of Gorafe loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 19.1-mile (30.7 km) trail through unique desert badlands.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks to look out for on Freila's cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy views of the expansive Negratín reservoir, often likened to the Colorado Canyon due to its surrounding badlands. The geological formations and deeply eroded, desert-like landscapes, particularly around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt;, are prominent. You might also spot traditional cave houses carved into hillsides in the villages.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Freila offers a good selection of challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059358742/hiking-cerro-jabalcon-from-zujar-around-the-embalse-de-negratin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking Cerro Jabalcón from Zújar around the Embalse de Negratín&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (19.1 km) path that includes significant ascents around Cerro Jabalcón. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31402018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert loop from Playa de Freila&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 34 miles (56 km) with considerable elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Freila?
A: The mystical reddish, desert tones of Freila's landscape are particularly striking around sunset, offering picturesque cycling opportunities. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the semi-desert climate suggests spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available, and many of the moderate routes could be suitable for families or beginners looking for a slightly longer ride. The varied terrain means you can find flatter sections within the fertile valleys, while more challenging ascents are typically found in the badlands or around mountains like Jabalcón.

Q: What amenities are available in Freila for touring cyclists?
A: Despite its rural setting, Freila provides essential facilities for visitors. You'll find supermarkets, banks, restaurants, and bars within the village. There are also options for accommodation, including a campsite and rural stays, catering to different preferences for overnight stops.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other activities in Freila?
A: Absolutely! The Negratín reservoir is a hub for water sports such as canoeing, sailing, swimming, and fishing, offering a refreshing break from cycling. It even features an inland beach at Cortijo del Cura. Additionally, you can explore the rich history of the area by visiting the ruins of Freila's 12th-13th century castle or Arabic watchtowers like Torre del Maruq.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: While specific routes aren't detailed to pass directly by historical sites, the region is rich in history. The village of Freila itself is known for its numerous cave houses and the ruins of its castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Freila – Zújar loop from Cerro Capallón&lt;/a&gt;, for example, starts near the castle, offering an opportunity to explore the historical context of the area.

Q: What makes the landscape around Freila unique for touring cyclists?
A: The landscape around Freila is unique due to its dramatic contrasts: small, fertile valleys with lush vegetation quickly give way to reddish wastelands and semi-desert badlands. This creates a visually stunning experience, with views of the expansive Negratín reservoir often framed by these arid, colorful hillsides. It's a geological spectacle that provides a constantly changing backdrop for your ride.
tips: The town of Zújar is a hidden gem in the middle of nature. Known for its impressive reservoir and its location on the slopes of the Sierra de Baza, Zújar is an ideal destination for those seeking tranquility and contact with nature. Its narrow, cobbled streets and its typical Andalusian white houses give it a special charm.
The is a hard-packed earth descent towards the Embalse de Negratin with great views as you go down. It's a pretty smooth surface most of the way, so you can concentrate on the views.
It is the most important monument in the city. It was built in the Nasrid period between the 13th and 15th centuries and consists of a tower, two sections of wall, a cistern and a parapet next to it.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar route typically starts and ends in Freila, a town known for its historical elements like the Castillo de Freila and unique cave houses. Parking is generally available within the town.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete, covering a distance of nearly 24.7 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness. While generally suitable for various skill levels, some segments are unpaved and can be more challenging. Be prepared for some portions where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, offering a continuous journey without needing to retrace your path.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter approximately 13.3 km of asphalt, 7.59 km of unpaved sections, and 3.80 km of paved areas. There's a compacted dirt descent towards the Negratín reservoir, which is mostly smooth.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to undertake this cycling tour are in autumn and spring. The region's continental climate makes these seasons ideal for enjoying the route comfortably.

faqs.question: What scenic views and natural features will I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning scenery, including excellent views of the Negratín Reservoir, often called 'El Mar del Altiplano.' The landscape features turquoise waters contrasted with reddish mountains and a semi-desert environment. You'll also get a View of Zújar and Cerro Jabalcón. The entire region is part of the UNESCO-recognized Granada Geopark, known for its unique 'badlands' geology with distinctive gullies.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on this public route, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with other cyclists, pedestrians, or wildlife. Be mindful of the varied terrain and potential for unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Embalse de Negratín — circular desde Freila por Zújar route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any opportunities for water sports or other activities near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Embalse de Negratín offers a range of water sports, including kayaking, paddle surfing, fishing, and swimming. There are well-equipped beaches near Freila where you can enjoy these activities.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camino Espiritual del Sur, Camino Cuesta de Baza, and Camino de Freila a Baza, among others.

waypoints.name: View of Zújar and Cerro Jabalcón
